fsm_model.description: Three-stage atomic write protocol enabling transactional updates to multiple attributes with all-or-nothing commit semantics. Protocol enforces client isolation, timeout-based resource management, and comprehensive validation before commit.

states.name: SERVER_NO_STATE
states.description: Server has no Atomic Write State associated with this client for this cluster/endpoint combination

states.name: SERVER_STATE_CREATED
states.description: Atomic Write State exists, server is buffering pending writes and enforcing timeout
invariants: atomic_writer_id is valid Operational Node ID

states.name: SERVER_COMMIT_VALIDATION
states.description: Server is validating all pending writes before attempting application

states.name: SERVER_COMMIT_APPLICATION
states.description: Server is attempting to apply all validated pending writes atomically

states.name: SERVER_ROLLBACK_IN_PROGRESS
states.description: Server is discarding pending writes in response to RollbackWrite request

states.name: CLIENT_IDLE
states.description: Client has no active atomic write transaction

states.name: CLIENT_AWAITING_BEGIN_RESPONSE
states.description: Client sent BeginWrite request and is waiting for server response

states.name: CLIENT_TRANSACTION_ACTIVE
states.description: Client has active transaction and can send write/read requests or commit/rollback

states.name: CLIENT_AWAITING_COMMIT_RESPONSE
states.description: Client sent CommitWrite and is waiting for server response

states.name: CLIENT_AWAITING_ROLLBACK_RESPONSE
states.description: Client sent RollbackWrite and is waiting for server response

states.name: CLIENT_TRANSACTION_COMPLETED
states.description: Transaction completed with success or failure status

transitions.guard_condition: response.StatusCode == FAILURE || response.StatusCode == INVALID_COMMAND || response.StatusCode == INVALID_IN_STATE
is_associated_with_state(client, current_state) && contains(attrib_ids_set, request.attribute_id) && can_buffer_write(request.attribute_id, request.value)

transitions.comment: Non-associated clients reading locked atomic attributes receive current committed value, not pending values from the transaction owning the lock

functions.name: atomic_writer_id_unavailable
functions.description: Checks if the Atomic Writer ID is unavailable in the current session context. Returns true if the session context does not provide a valid Atomic Writer ID.
functions.algorithm_detail: If session_context is Secure Session Context, extract Peer Node ID. If Peer Node ID is NULL or unavailable, return true. If session_context is Groupcast Session Context, return true (Groupcast makes Atomic Writer ID invalid). Otherwise return false.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T003 when validating BeginWrite request to reject requests without valid Atomic Writer ID

functions.name: is_valid_operational_node_id
functions.description: Validates that the provided ID is a valid Operational Node ID format within the accessing fabric.
functions.algorithm_detail: Check if id is within valid Operational Node ID range (non-zero, within fabric's node ID space, properly formatted as per Matter node ID specification). Return true if valid, false otherwise.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T003 when validating BeginWrite request to ensure Atomic Writer ID is properly formatted

functions.name: has_duplicates

parameters.name: list
parameters.description: List of attribute IDs to check

functions.description: Checks if the provided list contains any duplicate attribute IDs.
functions.algorithm_detail: Iterate through list and compare each element with all subsequent elements. If any element appears more than once, return true. Otherwise return false. Can also use set-based comparison: return list.size != unique(list).size.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T005 when validating AttributeRequests field in BeginWrite to reject requests with duplicate attributes

functions.name: contains_unsupported_attributes

parameters.name: attr_list
parameters.description: List of attribute IDs to validate

functions.description: Checks if any attribute ID in the list references an attribute not supported by the server cluster instance.
functions.algorithm_detail: For each attribute ID in attr_list, query the cluster instance's attribute registry. If any ID is not found in the registry, return true. Otherwise return false.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T006 when validating BeginWrite request to reject requests for non-existent attributes

functions.name: client_has_existing_state_on_same_cluster_endpoint
functions.description: Checks if the client already has an Atomic Write State associated with any attributes on the same cluster and endpoint combination.
functions.algorithm_detail: Query global Atomic Write State registry for any state where state.atomic_writer_id == client.atomic_writer_id AND state.accessing_fabric == client.accessing_fabric AND state.cluster_id == cluster_id AND state.endpoint_no == endpoint_no. Return true if any such state exists, false otherwise.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T007 when validating BeginWrite to prevent concurrent atomic writes on same cluster/endpoint

functions.name: all_basic_validations_passed
functions.description: Returns true if all basic validation checks (fabric, writer ID, empty list, duplicates, unsupported attributes) have passed.
functions.algorithm_detail: Return (accessing_fabric != NULL) AND is_valid_operational_node_id(atomic_writer_id) AND (AttributeRequests.size > 0) AND !has_duplicates(AttributeRequests) AND !contains_unsupported_attributes(AttributeRequests)
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T007, T008, T009, T010 as precondition for proceeding to attribute-level validation

functions.name: validate_all_attributes
functions.description: Performs per-attribute validation for atomic write eligibility. Returns list of status structures, one per attribute.
functions.algorithm_detail: For each attr_id in attr_list: (1) Check if client has write privilege using ACL - if not, status = UNSUPPORTED_ACCESS. (2) Check if attribute has Atomic quality - if not, status = INVALID_COMMAND. (3) Check if attribute is locked by different client's atomic write - if yes, status = BUSY. (4) Check if server has resources to buffer pending writes - if not, status = RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED. (5) Otherwise, status = SUCCESS. Return list of AtomicAttributeStatusStruct with AttributeID and StatusCode for each attribute.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T009 and T010 during BeginWrite processing to validate each requested attribute

functions.name: apply_shared_resource_expansion
functions.description: Expands attribute set if server requires additional attributes due to shared resource constraints. Returns potentially expanded list of attribute IDs.
functions.algorithm_detail: Check if any requested attribute belongs to a shared resource group that requires all group members to be included in atomic write. If yes, add all group members to the list and compute their validation statuses. Add corresponding AtomicAttributeStatusStruct entries to statuses parameter (passed by reference). Return expanded attribute list. If no expansion needed, return requested_attrs unchanged.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T009 and T010 after initial attribute validation to handle shared resource requirements

functions.name: any_attribute_validation_failed
functions.description: Returns true if validation of any attribute results in status other than SUCCESS.
functions.algorithm_detail: Call validate_all_attributes(attr_list) and check if any returned status is not SUCCESS. Return true if any non-SUCCESS status found, false if all SUCCESS.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T009 guard condition to determine if BeginWrite should return FAILURE response

functions.name: all_attribute_validations_passed
functions.description: Returns true if validation of all attributes results in SUCCESS status.
functions.algorithm_detail: Call validate_all_attributes(attr_list) and check if all returned statuses are SUCCESS. Return true only if all attributes have SUCCESS status.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T010 guard condition to determine if BeginWrite should create Atomic Write State

functions.name: negotiate_timeout

parameters.name: max_allowed
parameters.description: Maximum timeout server allows for this attribute set

functions.description: Negotiates effective timeout between client request and server maximum. Returns the timeout value to be used.
functions.algorithm_detail: If requested > max_allowed, return max_allowed. Otherwise return requested. This implements the rule: server MAY return max timeout if requested timeout exceeds maximum.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T010 when creating Atomic Write State to determine effective timeout value

functions.name: get_max_timeout_for_attributes

parameters.name: attr_set
parameters.description: Set of attributes for which timeout is needed

functions.description: Returns the maximum timeout the server allows for the given set of attributes.
functions.algorithm_detail: Query server configuration for timeout limits associated with each attribute or attribute group. Return the minimum of all applicable timeout limits, as the overall timeout must satisfy all attributes' constraints. Implementation-specific, may consider resource availability, attribute volatility, or cluster policy.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T010 as input to negotiate_timeout to determine server's maximum allowed timeout

functions.name: create_atomic_write_state

parameters.name: attrs
parameters.description: Set of attribute IDs in this atomic write

functions.description: Creates a new Atomic Write State associating the client with the specified attributes.
functions.algorithm_detail: Allocate new Atomic Write State structure in server memory. Set state.endpoint_no = endpoint, state.cluster_id = cluster, state.atomic_writer_id = writer_id, state.accessing_fabric = fabric, state.attrib_ids_set = attrs. Initialize state.pending_writes = empty map, state.timeout_timer = 0 (will be set by caller). Register state in global state registry indexed by (writer_id, fabric, cluster, endpoint). Lock all attributes in attrs to prevent other clients from creating atomic writes on them.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T010 action when BeginWrite succeeds to establish the Atomic Write State

functions.name: start_timeout_timer
functions.description: Initializes and starts the countdown timer for the Atomic Write State.
functions.algorithm_detail: Set timeout_timer = duration. Start system timer that decrements timeout_timer every millisecond. When timeout_timer reaches 0, trigger timeout_timer_expiry event (T023).
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T010 after creating Atomic Write State to enforce timeout-based rollback

functions.name: stop_timeout_timer
functions.description: Stops the countdown timer, preventing automatic timeout rollback.
functions.algorithm_detail: Cancel the system timer associated with timeout_timer. Set timer state to inactive so it no longer decrements or triggers expiry events.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T026 when CommitWrite is received to prevent timeout during commit processing

functions.name: send_atomic_response

parameters.name: status_code
parameters.description: Overall status code (SUCCESS, FAILURE, CONSTRAINT_ERROR, INVALID_COMMAND, INVALID_IN_STATE)

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Timeout value in milliseconds (0 if not applicable)

functions.description: Sends AtomicResponse message to client.
functions.algorithm_detail: Construct AtomicResponse message with StatusCode = status_code, AttributeStatus = attr_statuses. If status_code indicates BeginWrite response and status_code == SUCCESS, include Timeout field = timeout. Otherwise omit Timeout field. Send message via session layer to requesting client.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in multiple transitions (T002-T010, T015, T017, T025, T028-T033, T038-T039) to respond to AtomicRequest commands

functions.name: send_atomic_request

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Timeout value for BeginWrite, NULL for Commit/Rollback

functions.description: Client sends AtomicRequest message to server.
functions.algorithm_detail: Construct AtomicRequest message with RequestType = request_type, AttributeRequests = attrs. If request_type == BeginWrite, include Timeout field = timeout. Otherwise omit Timeout field. Send message via session layer to target server cluster/endpoint.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T001, T012, T024, T036, T037 when client initiates atomic write operations

functions.name: extract_attribute_ids_from_statuses
functions.description: Extracts the list of attribute IDs from AtomicAttributeStatusStruct list.
functions.algorithm_detail: Iterate through statuses list and extract AttributeID field from each AtomicAttributeStatusStruct. Return list of extracted attribute IDs in same order as statuses.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T011 and T012 to extract accepted attributes from BeginWrite response

functions.name: client_rejects_statuses_or_timeout
functions.description: Determines if client accepts or rejects the attribute statuses or alternative timeout returned by server.
functions.algorithm_detail: Client-side decision logic. Check if any attribute has unacceptable status (e.g., BUSY, RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED when client wants guaranteed immediate access). Check if response.Timeout is significantly lower than requested timeout and insufficient for client's needs. Return true if client decides to reject and cancel atomic write, false if client accepts conditions.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T012 guard to determine if client should send RollbackWrite after receiving BeginWrite response

functions.name: client_rejects_conditions
functions.description: Determines if client rejects the conditions and decides to cancel atomic write.
functions.algorithm_detail: Client-side decision based on application logic, received error codes, timeout values, or user input. Return true if client chooses to cancel, false otherwise.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T037 guard to determine if client should send RollbackWrite after BeginWrite phase

functions.name: is_associated_with_state
functions.description: Checks if the client is associated with the given Atomic Write State.
functions.algorithm_detail: Return (client.atomic_writer_id == state.atomic_writer_id) AND (client.accessing_fabric == state.accessing_fabric). This ensures both writer ID and fabric match for proper isolation.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T015, T016, T017, T019, T020 to validate that write/read requests come from the client owning the Atomic Write State

functions.name: contains
functions.description: Checks if the attribute ID is in the set.
functions.algorithm_detail: Standard set membership test. Return true if attr is in set, false otherwise.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T015-T017, T019-T021 to verify that requested attribute is part of the Atomic Write State

functions.name: can_buffer_write
functions.description: Determines if the write can potentially succeed during commit. Returns false if write will definitely fail.
functions.algorithm_detail: Perform preliminary validation: check data type compatibility, range constraints, value format. Check if the write would require resources that are guaranteed to be unavailable at commit (e.g., list would exceed maximum size). Return true if write could potentially succeed, false if it will definitely fail. This allows server to provide early error feedback without waiting for commit.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T016 and T017 guards to determine if write should be buffered or rejected immediately

functions.name: buffer_pending_write
functions.description: Stores the pending write in the Atomic Write State's buffer, replacing any previous pending write to the same attribute.
functions.algorithm_detail: Store or update pending_writes[attr_id] = value in the Atomic Write State's pending write map. If attr_id already has a pending write, replace it with the new value. Ensure write is isolated and not visible to other clients or server operations.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T016 action when server accepts write request

functions.name: determine_write_error
functions.description: Determines the appropriate error code for a write that cannot be buffered.
functions.algorithm_detail: Analyze why the write would fail: type mismatch → INVALID_DATA_TYPE, out of range → CONSTRAINT_ERROR, insufficient resources → RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED, etc. Return the most specific applicable error code from Matter status code enumeration.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T017 action to generate appropriate error response for rejected write

functions.name: send_write_request
functions.description: Client sends write request for a single attribute within an active atomic transaction.
functions.algorithm_detail: Construct Write Request message with attribute_id = attr_id and attribute_value = value. Send via session layer to server. Message is implicitly associated with the active Atomic Write State via session context.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T014 action when client writes attribute during transaction

functions.name: send_write_response
functions.description: Server sends write response acknowledging or rejecting the write request.
functions.algorithm_detail: Construct Write Response message with status_code = status and attribute_id = attr_id. Send via session layer back to requesting client.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T015-T017 actions to respond to client write requests

functions.name: send_read_request
functions.description: Client sends read request for an attribute within active atomic transaction.
functions.algorithm_detail: Construct Read Request message with attribute_id = attr_id. Send via session layer to server. Request is implicitly associated with active Atomic Write State via session context.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T018 action when client reads attribute during transaction

functions.name: has_pending_write_for_client
functions.description: Checks if the client has a pending write for the specified attribute in its Atomic Write State.
functions.algorithm_detail: Look up client's Atomic Write State. Check if pending_writes map contains entry for attr_id. Return true if pending write exists, false otherwise.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T019 guard to determine if server should return pending value or current value

functions.name: get_pending_value

parameters.name: client
parameters.description: Client that wrote the pending value

functions.description: Retrieves the pending (uncommitted) value for an attribute from client's Atomic Write State.
functions.algorithm_detail: Look up client's Atomic Write State. Return pending_writes[attr_id]. This value is the uncommitted write that only the owning client can observe.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T019 action to return pending value to client that wrote it

functions.name: get_current_value
functions.description: Retrieves the current committed value of an attribute from server storage.
functions.algorithm_detail: Query server's attribute storage for attr_id and return its current value. This is the value visible to all clients except those with pending writes to this attribute in their Atomic Write State.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T020 action to return current value to client that hasn't written to the attribute

functions.name: send_read_response
functions.description: Server sends read response with attribute value to client.
functions.algorithm_detail: Construct Read Response message with status_code = status, attribute_id = attr_id, and attribute_value = value. Send via session layer back to requesting client.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T019 and T020 actions to respond to client read requests

functions.name: modifies_atomic_attribute
functions.description: Determines if the command would modify the specified attribute.
functions.algorithm_detail: Analyze command's behavior specification. Check if command writes to, updates, or otherwise modifies attr_id. Return true if command affects the attribute, false otherwise. This requires knowledge of command semantics from cluster specification.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T021 guard to detect external modifications to attributes locked in Atomic Write State

functions.name: apply_command_immediately
functions.description: Executes the command immediately, modifying attributes as specified, regardless of atomic write state.
functions.algorithm_detail: Execute command according to its cluster specification. Apply all attribute modifications to server storage immediately. This bypasses atomic write isolation, as spec requires: 'it SHALL apply these changes as if there were no atomic write'.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T021 action when external command modifies attribute during atomic write

functions.name: update_data_version
functions.description: Increments or updates the data version for the attribute after external modification.
functions.algorithm_detail: Increment data_version_tracking[attr_id] counter or update version timestamp. This version change will be detected during commit validation (T028) and cause DATA_VERSION_MISMATCH error.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T021 action to track external modifications that will invalidate pending atomic writes

functions.name: mark_potential_commit_failure

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: State that may fail at commit

functions.description: Marks the Atomic Write State as potentially failing at commit due to external modification.
functions.algorithm_detail: Set flag in state indicating that validation should check for data version mismatches or other conflicts introduced by external commands. This is informational to help commit validation detect issues.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T021 action to track that external command may have invalidated pending writes

functions.name: discard_all_pending_writes

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: State whose pending writes should be discarded

functions.description: Discards all pending writes in the Atomic Write State without applying them.
functions.algorithm_detail: Clear pending_writes map. Free any associated memory or resources. Ensure no side effects from discarded writes (they were never applied to server state).
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T023, T028, T029, T039 during rollback or commit failure to discard uncommitted changes

functions.name: discard_atomic_write_state
functions.description: Deletes the Atomic Write State, releasing all associated resources and locks.
functions.algorithm_detail: Remove state from global state registry. Release locks on all attributes in state.attrib_ids_set, making them available for other atomic writes. Free state structure memory. Stop timeout timer if still active.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T023, T028, T029, T032, T033, T039 to clean up state after transaction completion

functions.name: received_commit_request_since_creation
functions.description: Checks if a CommitWrite request has been received since the Atomic Write State was created.
functions.algorithm_detail: Check state flag or event history to determine if CommitWrite message was received. Return true if commit was received (even if currently being processed), false if timeout should trigger rollback.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T023 guard to prevent timeout rollback if commit is in progress

functions.name: state_matches
functions.description: Verifies that the CommitWrite or RollbackWrite request matches the client's Atomic Write State.
functions.algorithm_detail: Return (client.atomic_writer_id == state.atomic_writer_id) AND (client.accessing_fabric == state.accessing_fabric) AND (state.endpoint_no == endpoint) AND (state.cluster_id == cluster) AND (set_equal_irrespective_of_order(state.attrib_ids_set, attrs)). The attribute set comparison must be order-independent per spec.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T025, T026, T038, T039 to validate that Commit/Rollback request matches the state

functions.name: validate_all_pending_writes_with_context

parameters.name: pending_writes
parameters.description: Map of pending writes to validate

parameters.name: attrs
parameters.description: Full set of attributes in Atomic Write State

functions.description: Validates all pending writes in the context of each other and current server state. Returns status for each attribute that would be modified.
functions.algorithm_detail: For each pending write: (1) Check if data version has changed since BeginWrite (external command modified it) - if yes, status = DATA_VERSION_MISMATCH. (2) Evaluate integrity checks using pending values of all attributes in attrs as context (per spec: 'integrity checks SHALL be evaluated in the context of the pending values'). (3) Check for constraint violations within this single write. (4) If all checks pass, status = SUCCESS. Return list of AtomicAttributeStatusStruct for each modified attribute.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T027 action during commit validation phase

functions.name: any_validation_failed
functions.description: Checks if any attribute validation resulted in a non-SUCCESS status.
functions.algorithm_detail: Iterate through statuses list. Return true if any AtomicAttributeStatusStruct has StatusCode != SUCCESS. Return false if all statuses are SUCCESS.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T028 and T029 guards to determine commit outcome based on validation results

functions.name: would_violate_constraints
functions.description: Checks if applying all pending writes collectively would violate any cross-attribute constraints.
functions.algorithm_detail: Evaluate cluster-level constraints and invariants that span multiple attributes. Create temporary view of attribute state with pending writes applied. Check if this view violates any cluster constraints, invariants, or business logic rules. Return true if collective writes violate constraints (even though individual writes may be valid), false if constraints satisfied.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T029 guard to detect collective constraint violations that individual validations miss

functions.name: apply_all_pending_writes_atomically
functions.description: Attempts to apply all pending writes atomically to server storage. Returns status for each write attempted.
functions.algorithm_detail: Begin atomic transaction at storage layer (if supported). For each (attr_id, value) in pending_writes: attempt to write value to attr_id's storage location, store returned status code. If any write fails: rollback all writes in this transaction, return list with failure statuses. If all writes succeed: commit transaction, return list with all SUCCESS statuses. Process writes as if they arrived in a single message per spec requirement.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T031 action during commit application phase

functions.name: any_application_failed
functions.description: Checks if any write application resulted in failure.
functions.algorithm_detail: Iterate through results list. Return true if any AtomicAttributeStatusStruct has StatusCode != SUCCESS. Return false if all statuses are SUCCESS.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in T032 and T033 guards to determine final commit outcome

definitions.definition: An Operational Node ID that identifies the client within the accessing fabric. For Secure Session Context, it is the Peer Node ID. For Groupcast Session Context, it is invalid. Must be a valid Operational Node ID to be used in atomic writes.
definitions.security_relevance: Ensures client identity and prevents unauthorized clients from accessing or interfering with atomic write transactions. Foundation for transaction isolation and access control.

definitions.definition: Server-side data structure that associates a client (via Atomic Writer ID and fabric) with a set of attributes on a cluster/endpoint. Contains: endpoint_no, cluster_id, atomic_writer_id, accessing_fabric, attrib_ids_set, pending_writes map, timeout value, and timeout timer. Enforces transaction isolation and resource management.
definitions.security_relevance: Core isolation mechanism preventing concurrent atomic writes from interfering. Ensures only the owning client can write to locked attributes until commit or rollback.

definitions.definition: Uncommitted write buffered in Atomic Write State. Visible only to the client that created it when reading. Not visible to other clients or server operations. Applied atomically during commit or discarded during rollback.
definitions.security_relevance: Implements transaction isolation and confidentiality. Prevents information leakage of uncommitted data and ensures atomicity guarantees.

definitions.definition: Attribute property indicating the attribute supports atomic write protocol. Only attributes with Atomic quality can participate in atomic writes.
definitions.security_relevance: Prevents protocol misuse by restricting atomic writes to attributes designed for transactional semantics.

definitions.definition: Fabric identifier from session context that scopes the atomic write. Must be present for all atomic write operations. Part of Atomic Write State identity.
definitions.security_relevance: Enforces multi-tenancy isolation. Prevents cross-fabric atomic write interference and ensures fabric-level access control.

definitions.definition: Server behavior where additional attributes beyond client's request are included in atomic write due to internal resource constraints. Server returns statuses for all included attributes. Client must use expanded set in subsequent Commit/Rollback requests.
definitions.security_relevance: Can increase transaction scope and resource consumption. Must be transparently communicated to client to prevent protocol state mismatches.

definitions.definition: Error condition when an attribute's version changes between BeginWrite and CommitWrite, typically due to external command modification. Causes commit validation to fail with DATA_VERSION_MISMATCH error.
definitions.security_relevance: Prevents lost updates and ensures commit operates on consistent data. Critical for maintaining causality and data integrity.

definitions.definition: Error condition when pending writes collectively violate cluster constraints or invariants, even if individual writes are valid. Results in CONSTRAINT_ERROR status and complete transaction rollback.
definitions.security_relevance: Enforces cluster-level invariants and business logic. Prevents atomic writes from creating inconsistent or invalid cluster state.

definitions.definition: Automatic rollback mechanism triggered when timeout timer expires before client sends CommitWrite. Server discards pending writes and Atomic Write State without sending response.
definitions.security_relevance: Prevents indefinite resource holding and ensures timely release of attribute locks. Critical for availability and denial-of-service prevention.

definitions.definition: Atomic write flow consisting of: (1) BeginWrite - establish state and lock attributes, (2) Write Requests - buffer pending changes, (3) CommitWrite or RollbackWrite - apply all or discard all changes.
definitions.security_relevance: Structured protocol ensures proper transaction lifecycle, validation points, and deterministic outcomes. Prevents partial updates and state corruption.

security_properties.description: Only clients with valid Atomic Writer ID from Secure Session Context can initiate atomic writes. Groupcast sessions cannot perform atomic writes.
All atomic write operations must have accessing fabric. States are scoped to fabric, preventing cross-fabric interference.
Only the client associated with an Atomic Write State (matching writer ID and fabric) can write to its attributes.
Pending writes are visible only to the owning client. Other clients and server operations see current committed values.
During commit, either all pending writes succeed and are applied, or all fail and none are applied. No partial commits allowed.
If client does not commit before timeout, server automatically rolls back pending writes and discards state.
Each attribute's write privilege is checked during BeginWrite. Attributes requiring higher privilege return UNSUPPORTED_ACCESS.
Attributes in one client's atomic write cannot be included in another client's atomic write (BUSY status).
Server rejects attribute inclusion if insufficient resources to buffer pending writes (RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED).
Collective pending writes must satisfy cluster constraints. Violations cause complete rollback with CONSTRAINT_ERROR.
External commands that modify attributes during atomic write cause commit to fail with DATA_VERSION_MISMATCH, preventing lost updates.
Client cannot have multiple concurrent Atomic Write States on same cluster/endpoint. Prevents deadlocks and state confusion.
Atomic Write State is always discarded after commit or rollback, releasing locks and freeing resources.
Integrity checks during validation use pending values of all attributes in state as context, not just current values.
Clients without Atomic Write State read committed values only, never pending uncommitted values from other transactions (PROP_043).

fsm_model.initial_state: SERVER_NO_STATE for server, CLIENT_IDLE for client
fsm_model.note_on_read_semantics: Read requests are handled differently based on client association: T019 (associated client with pending write), T020 (associated client without pending write), T021a (non-associated client reading locked attribute)

security_assumptions.assumption: Session layer correctly derives Atomic Writer ID from Peer Node ID in Secure Session Context and prevents forgery
security_assumptions.if_violated: Attacker could impersonate clients, hijack atomic writes, bypass fabric isolation, and corrupt cluster state

security_assumptions.assumption: Session layer correctly associates and enforces accessing fabric for all operations
security_assumptions.if_violated: Cross-fabric attacks become possible, breaking multi-tenancy isolation and allowing unauthorized data access

security_assumptions.assumption: Server has sufficient memory and resources to buffer pending writes for reasonable transaction sizes
security_assumptions.if_violated: Atomic writes would be rejected with RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED, causing denial-of-service. System could crash under load

security_assumptions.assumption: Timeout mechanism is reliable and server can accurately detect and act on timer expiry
security_assumptions.if_violated: Stale transactions would hold locks indefinitely, causing resource exhaustion and denial-of-service

security_assumptions.assumption: Data version tracking accurately detects external modifications to attributes
security_assumptions.if_violated: Lost updates could occur where atomic writes overwrite external changes without detection, violating causality

security_assumptions.assumption: Pending write buffer is implemented with proper isolation (memory protection, access control)
security_assumptions.if_violated: Pending values could leak to unauthorized clients, breaking confidentiality and transaction isolation

security_assumptions.assumption: Integrity checks and constraint validators are comprehensive and correctly implemented
security_assumptions.if_violated: Invalid data could be committed, corrupting cluster state and breaking system invariants

security_assumptions.assumption: Attribute locking mechanism prevents concurrent access by different clients
security_assumptions.if_violated: Race conditions and deadlocks could occur, breaking isolation and atomicity guarantees

security_assumptions.assumption: Server correctly identifies commands that modify attributes and updates versions appropriately
security_assumptions.if_violated: External modifications might not be detected, causing lost updates or stale atomic writes to be applied

security_assumptions.assumption: Storage layer supports atomic commit/rollback operations (no partial persistence of writes)
security_assumptions.if_violated: Partial commits could occur at storage layer, breaking ACID properties and causing data corruption

validation_checklist.cryptographic_operations_detailed: N/A - no cryptographic operations in this protocol section
validation_checklist.coverage_note: All specification behaviors covered including reads from non-associated clients (T021a) per section 7.15.3
